description: Issue #205 Cat 8: Improve content stream error recovery for malformed PDFs

userStories.title: Implement graceful content stream error recovery
userStories.description: issue-1181.pdf and issue-848.pdf fail with content stream parsing errors. The current parser aborts on malformed operators instead of recovering. Implement graceful degradation: skip malformed tokens/operators and continue extracting what can be parsed, rather than failing the entire page.
acceptanceCriteria: issue-1181.pdf no longer produces a parse error; extracts available chars
issue-848.pdf no longer produces a parse error; extracts available chars
Content stream parser skips malformed operators and continues
A warning/diagnostic is emitted for skipped operators (via tracing)
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs (content stream execution), crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/content_stream.rs (tokenization). The parser should catch errors at the operator level and skip to the next recognizable operator. Common malformed patterns: unexpected '<<' in content stream, invalid operand types, unknown operators, truncated streams. Implement a try-recover loop: try parsing next operator, on error skip bytes until next recognizable operator token. Python pdfplumber (via pdfminer) handles this by silently ignoring unknown operators.
